incorrects.description: Student selected 1 (counted items instead of containers)

params.description: Boxes themselves highlight to distinguish them from items inside.

steps.text: Count the BOXES, not what's inside them. How many boxes do you see?
Let's count together. Watch the boxes light up: 1, 2. How many boxes?

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 (counted items per container instead of containers)

params.description: Box outlines highlight to distinguish containers from contents.

steps.text: You're counting what's INSIDE. First, count just the boxes. The containers themselves.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Boxes highlight sequentially: 1, 2. System demonstrates counting.

steps.text: Here, let me show you. I count the boxes, not what's inside. 1, 2. Two boxes. Those are the groups. When we count groups, we count the containers first. The answer is 2.

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 (counted groups instead of items per group)

params.description: First box zooms or highlights. Items inside become focal.

steps.text: Now look INSIDE one box. Count what you see in that one box, not the boxes themselves.

params.description: Items in one box highlight sequentially: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

steps.text: Let's count the items in one box together: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. How many in each box?

incorrects.description: Student selected 10 (counted all items across all groups)

params.description: First box zooms or highlights to focus on just one group.

steps.text: You counted everything. Look at just ONE box. How many items are in that single box?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: One box highlights. Items inside are counted sequentially: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5.

steps.text: Let me walk you through it. I pick one box and count what's inside: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Each box has 5 items. That's what we mean by 'in each group.' The answer is 5.

incorrects.description: Student selected 5 groups of 2 (reversed groups and items per group)

steps.text: You switched them. First number is the GROUPS. How many boxes? Then look inside ONE box. How many in each?

incorrects.description: Student selected 2 groups of 10 (correct group count, but counted all items as items per group)

params.description: One box zooms. Items inside that box highlight.

steps.text: You got the groups right: 2 boxes. But now count just ONE box. Don't count both boxes together.

incorrects.description: Student selected 10 groups of 2 (counted total items as groups)

params.description: Box outlines highlight to distinguish containers from items.

steps.text: Groups are the CONTAINERS. Count the boxes first, not the items. How many boxes?

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Boxes highlight sequentially (1, 2), then one box zooms and items inside count (1, 2, 3, 4, 5).

steps.text: Let me show you the whole process. First, I count the boxes: 1, 2. Two groups. Then I look inside one box and count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5. Five in each. So I have 2 groups of 5. That's how we name equal groups: groups first, then how many in each. The answer is 2 groups of 5.
